target.name: Cognition
target.founder: Scott Wu, Steven Hao, Walden Yan

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $12.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Cognition is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Scott Wu, Steven Hao, and Walden Yan that has raised $175M+. The peer universe comprises five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables spanning Seed and Series A stages, with one public reference point (Palantir, ticker PLTR) and eight private frontier-tech peers including Thinking Machines Lab, Liquid AI, Field AI, Sakana AI, and Twelve Labs.

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method, using total raised ($175M+) as a proxy for implied Seed-stage base value of $5M, applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier to derive an implied valuation of $12M. The Peer-Median method examines five same-sector comparables with valuations ranging from $250M (Twelve Labs, Series A) to $12.0B (Thinking Machines Lab, Seed), yielding a median of $2.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a range of $10M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.0B. The wide dispersion—spanning nearly three orders of magnitude—reflects acute uncertainty inherent in early-stage frontier AI valuations where comparable companies exhibit heterogeneous funding patterns and stage classifications. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weight to assign the Thinking Machines Lab outlier at $12B and whether Cognition's funding density ($175M) and founder background justify positioning nearer the median ($1.0B) or upper bound ($2.3B) of the range.
